title: Smoky Creamy Italian Potato Soup
description: A low‑calorie Italian‑inspired potato soup with smoky paprika, creamy yogurt, and tangy pickled red onions for a flavorful monsoon twist.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add diced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in smoked paprika, coating the onions and garlic, and cook for 30 seconds.
Add diced potatoes, vegetable broth, and milk; bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook until potatoes are tender, about 15–18 minutes.
Whisk in Dijon mustard and chopped pickled red onions, ensuring they are well distributed.
Once potatoes are tender, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup to a creamy consistency (blend about half of the soup if preferred).
Stir in low‑fat Greek yogurt, heating gently but not boiling to keep the yogurt from curdling; season with salt and pepper to taste.
Ladle soup into bowls, garnish with fresh basil, and serve hot.
Optional: drizzle a pinch of smoked paprika or chili flakes for extra heat.
